rulings.Case Title: BOSTON PROPERTIES INC A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al VS. WILLY BIETAK PRODUCTIONS INC A NEVADA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 6, 2008 LINE 4. PLAINTIFF BOSTON PROPERTIES, LIMITED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE BARRY DECLARATION, COUPLED WITH THE FRANK DEPOSTION TESTIMONY, CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS THE PARTIES'S UNDERSTANDING OF HOW THE CHARGES FOR THE ZAMBONI MACHINE WERE TO BE DETERMINED. THE FOLOWING OBJECTIONS TO THE WAINESS DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED: 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 AND 12. NUMBER TWO IS OVERRULED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: BOSTON PROPERTIES INC A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al VS. WILLY BIETAK PRODUCTIONS INC A NEVADA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 6, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANTS WILLY BIETAK PRODUCTIONS, INC. AND WILLY BIETAK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE SEVENTH AND NINETH CAUSES OF ACTION IS GRANTED. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF AN INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION OR CONCEALMENT ON THE PART OF DEFENDANTS. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO THE PARTIES' UNDERSTANDING OF THE CALCULATION OF RENT FOR THE ZAMBONI MACHINE. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MARY M CHIARENZA VS. ROSARIO J CHIARENZA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 6, 2008 LINE 6. CROSS-DEFENDANT MARY CHIARENZA'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE DEMURRER IS BASED ON THE CONTENTION THAT GRACE CHRISTEN'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT-IN-INTERVENTION IS DEFECTIVE. THE COURT OVERRULED THE DEMURRER TO THE CHRISTEN FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT-IN-INTERVENTION. FOR THE SAME REASONS, IT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER. TEN DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN HANNON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, OFFICER JOHN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 6, 2008 LINE 9. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND OFFICER JOHN RUGGEIRO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT: THE DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION TWO THROUGH EIGHT, THE STATE LAW CLAIMS, IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES DAMAGE IN 2005 FROM ACTS COMMENCING IN 2004. THE TWO YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS HAS RUN ON PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILED TO FILE CLAIMS WITHIN SIX MONTHS OF THE DENIAL OF THE CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF'S STATE LAW CLAIMS FAIL TO IDENTIFY A STATUTORY BASIS FOR THE CLAIMS. THE CONDUCT ALLEGED AS TO OFFICER RUGGERIO IS PRIVILEGED. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE FEDERAL CLAIMS IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. UNDER SECTION 1983, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD WHAT CONSTITUTIONAL RIGHT WAS VIOLATED. AS TO THE A.D.A. CLAIM, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE HOW DEFENDANT DENIED PLAINTIFF ANY BENEFITS. =(302/PJM)
